slides.title: Welcome to JC Bitcoin
slides.subtitle: Monthly Socratic Seminar
slides.footer: Jersey City Bitcoin • Every 3rd Wednesday • jcbitcoin.org

slides.title: What is Bitcoin?
bullets: A peer-to-peer electronic cash system
Created in 2009 by Satoshi Nakamoto
Open-source, decentralized, and permissionless
Fixed supply of 21 million bitcoin

slides.title: Why Bitcoin Matters (Stress Test)
bullets: Censorship-resistant money that no government can freeze
Borderless payments settled in minutes, not days
Self-custody: you hold your own keys, you hold your own money
Transparent monetary policy enforced by code
Growing adoption by institutions, nations, and individuals
The hardest money ever created — harder than gold

slides.title: Key Concepts
bullets: Blockchain: a chain of blocks containing transaction data
Mining: the process of securing the network and issuing new bitcoin

slides.title: The Bitcoin Network
bullets: Thousands of nodes validate every transaction independently
Miners compete to find the next block using proof-of-work
Difficulty adjusts every 2,016 blocks (~2 weeks) to maintain 10-minute target
Block reward halves every 210,000 blocks (~4 years)

slides.title: Getting Your First Bitcoin
bullets: Choose a reputable exchange or peer-to-peer marketplace
Complete identity verification if required
Start small — you can buy a fraction of a bitcoin
Transfer to your own wallet for self-custody

slides.title: Questions & Discussion
bullets: What surprised you most about Bitcoin?
What concerns do you still have?
How would you explain Bitcoin to a friend?
